questions.question: Какую проблему решает GIC (FLIP-158)?

options.text: Даже с async + incremental checkpoint duration NONDETERMINISTIC — зависит от RocksDB flush, compaction, network spikes. p50=30s, p99=10min. Это catastrophic для EOS Kafka pipelines (transaction.timeout.ms). GIC даёт STABLE seconds checkpoint duration независимо от state size — каждый state mutation дубль-пишется в DSTL (append-only log на DFS), checkpoint = flush DSTL (ms). Materialization separate periodic cycle

questions.explanation: GIC основная проблема: checkpoint duration variability. Без GIC даже c incremental — duration depends on RocksDB internal state (flush cycle, compaction storms, network). Distribution: p50=30s, p99=10min, occasional minutes. На EOS Kafka pipelines это catastrophic — transaction.timeout.ms должен быть > worst case checkpoint duration. С GIC duration становится STABLE: p50=2s, p99=10s independent от state size (TB or 100GB — почти same). Mechanism: каждый state.update() дубль-пишется в DSTL log (DFS append-only) + underlying RocksDB. Checkpoint = small DSTL flush (recent mutations). Materialization = periodic RocksDB snapshot upload (10 min default). State в DFS = materialization + DSTL.

questions.question: Что такое DSTL и как работает recovery с GIC?

options.text: DSTL = Distributed State Local — local cache

DSTL = Durable Short-term Log. Append-only log на DFS. Каждая state mutation appended (PUT/DELETE/ADD entries). Flush на checkpoint (small DFS write — ms). Recovery: load materialized state (RocksDB snapshot от last materialization) + replay DSTL entries since materialization. Recovery time bounded by materialization interval × write_rate / DFS_bandwidth — predictable

questions.explanation: DSTL (Durable Short-term Log) — append-only log on DFS, central GIC mechanism. Each entry contains stateName, key, operation (PUT/DELETE/ADD), value. Sequential append. Buffer ~64KB in memory, flush on checkpoint trigger или periodic (1s). Flush — small DFS write (recent mutations only), milliseconds. Recovery: (1) Load materialization (full RocksDB snapshot from last materialization point); (2) Replay DSTL entries from materialization sequence start к checkpoint sequence end. Recovery time = state_size / DFS_bandwidth + DSTL_replay (bounded by materialization_interval × write_rate). Default 10 min interval × 10K writes/sec = 6M entries × 200 bytes = 1.2GB additional replay. Negligible vs state size.

questions.question: Cost / trade-offs GIC?

options.text: GIC trades steady-state DFS bandwidth (always writing DSTL ~10-30% bandwidth overhead) for stable checkpoint duration. Per-mutation latency increase 10-30% (RocksDB write + DSTL append). DFS storage слегка больше (DSTL log + materializations). Materialization storms (concurrent с checkpoint) могут double DFS bandwidth temporarily. Skip GIC для small state (< 10GB) где overhead не paid back
GIC может быть disabled, no cost

questions.explanation: GIC trade-offs: (1) Steady-state DFS bandwidth — DSTL always writing, +10-30% bandwidth vs incremental-only. На 100K mutations/sec × 200 bytes = 20 MB/s sustained DFS write per operator. S3 PUT costs noticeable; (2) Per-mutation latency — каждый state.update() now writes к DSTL too. Batching в 64KB buffers minimizes, but +10-30% mutation latency typical. На latency-critical processing measurable; (3) DFS storage — DSTL log retention + materializations. Slightly больше overall; (4) Materialization storms — operators materialize on independent schedule (jitter), but coincident с checkpoint trigger possible → temporary DFS bandwidth doubling; (5) Recovery время slightly higher due к DSTL replay (but bounded). When skip GIC: small state (<10GB), latency-critical paths, DFS bandwidth cost prohibitive, mostly read-only workload. When mandatory: TB state, EOS Kafka, bursty backpressure, frequent rescale.

questions.question: GIC и incremental checkpoints (RocksDB native) — alternatives, нельзя использовать вместе.
questions.explanation: False. GIC и incremental checkpoints COMPLEMENT друг друга, ENABLED ВМЕСТЕ — standard production setup. Architecture: ChangelogStateBackend (GIC) WRAPS RocksDB (incremental). DSTL handles checkpoint cadence (frequent, stable, small flushes). RocksDB native snapshots handle materializations (periodic, every 10 min, incremental — only new SSTs uploaded). Materialization itself uses incremental — diff vs previous materialization, only new SSTs uploaded. So GIC + incremental: (a) Frequent checkpoints (30-60s) — fast DSTL flushes regardless of underlying state; (b) Periodic materializations (10 min) — efficient incremental SST uploads, not full state every time; (c) Result: stable fast checkpoints (GIC) + efficient long-term storage (incremental). Configuration: state.backend.incremental: true + state.backend.changelog.enabled: true. Both should be enabled для production TB-scale state.
